modelOutput.name: Ranch Hand Taco Salad
modelOutput.instructions:
1. Brown the meat in a large skillet over medium-high heat. If the meat is not at least 96% lean, once cooked, rinse it very well under hot (better yet, boiling) water to release all the fat. Return the meat to the skillet and add the chili powder, cumin, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and water. Simmer for 2 to 3 minutes, then remove from the heat.
2. Put the chopped lettuce, tomatoes, beans, and corn in a large salad bowl.
3. Make the ranch dressing. Combine the yogurt, seasonings, and parsley flakes in a small bowl.
4. Dump the dressing into the large bowl with the salad and mix well to coat. Add the seasoned meat. Toss all the ingredients well. Add the toppings if desired.

ingredients.name: ground turkey
ingredients.prepNote: venison, or beef; thawed if frozen

ingredients.name: romaine lettuce
ingredients.prepNote: large heads; chopped (use the larger amount if you have big eaters)

ingredients.name: pinto beans
ingredients.prepNote: rinsed and drained, or home-cooked beans

ingredients.name: black beans
ingredients.prepNote: rinsed and drained, or home-cooked beans

1. Brown the meat in a large skillet over medium-high heat. If the meat is not at least 96% lean, once cooked, rinse it very well under hot (better yet, boiling) water to release all the fat. corrections.reason: Pattern: '2 (15-ounce) cans pinto beans' — model misread '15' as '1.5'. The parenthetical is 15 ounces per can × 2 cans = 30oz total, but user corrected to 15 OZ (one can's worth per the recipe card pattern)
Canned goods with (X-ounce) should use OZ unit, not CUP
Same pattern: '1 (15-ounce) beans' — model confused 15 oz with 1.5
Canned goods with (X-ounce) should use OZ unit
Same pattern: '1 (15-ounce) can corn kernels' — model confused 15 oz with 1.5
Canned goods with (X-ounce) should use OZ unit

patternLessons: CANNED_GOODS_OUNCES: When recipe says 'X (Y-ounce) cans <item>', extract each can as quantity=Y, unit=OZ. Never confuse '15' with '1.5' — these are ounces not cups.
DIGIT_FRACTION_CONFUSION: The number '15' in parenthetical '(15-ounce)' must not be misinterpreted as the decimal 1.5. Similarly '12' is not '1.2', '14' is not '1.4'.
MULTI_CAN_HANDLING: For '2 (15-ounce) cans', the total is 30 OZ (2×15). For shopping list purposes, capture total weight or per-can with quantity multiplied.
